stories.title: Tax advisers: one month left to register under new rules
stories.summary: HMRC has issued a reminder that tax advisers have one month left to register under new rules requiring professional registration. The deadline marks a significant shift in how unregulated tax advice will be overseen in the UK.

stories.title: HMRC Drafts Rules To Collect More Financial Tax Data
stories.summary: HMRC has issued draft regulations that would expand data collection requirements on interest income and card sales from banks. The move is aimed at closing the tax gap, which currently stands at 6.4% (£59.2 billion) for 2024–25.

stories.title: HMRC charts growing use of tech in transformation roadmap update
stories.summary: HMRC has published its first transformation roadmap update, cataloguing its growing use of AI and cloud computing as it works toward becoming 'more efficient, modernised and automated'. The roadmap outlines ambitions for digital-first tax compliance.

stories.title: Shabana Mahmood retained as Home Secretary under new PM Andy Burnham
stories.summary: Incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham confirmed Shabana Mahmood's continuation as Home Secretary, signalling continuity in immigration policy. Burnham has backed Mahmood's immigration crackdown despite pressure from Labour backbenchers.

stories.title: 10 arrested in UK immigration enforcement raids at Evri depots
stories.summary: UK Immigration Enforcement officers carried out coordinated operations at Evri delivery depots across England, arresting 10 individuals on suspicion of immigration offences. The raids were part of ongoing efforts to tackle illegal working in the logistics sector.

stories.title: UK ETA errors continue to disrupt travellers
stories.summary: Reports highlight ongoing issues with the UK's Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) system, with errors leaving travellers unable to board flights to the UK. The Home Office system has faced criticism since its wider rollout.

stories.title: Ed Miliband appointed as UK Foreign Secretary
stories.summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham has appointed Ed Miliband as the UK's Foreign Secretary, a surprise appointment that signals a significant shift in the FCDO's leadership. Miliband called the role an 'honour of my life'.

stories.title: New PM needs urgent Foreign Office decisions on aid budget
stories.summary: The Independent reports that PM Burnham and his new Foreign Secretary face immediate decisions over how to deliver foreign policy and development ambitions in the wake of previous spending cuts to the UK aid budget. Development organisations have urged the new FCDO to reverse recent cuts.

stories.title: Bond reacts to Ed Miliband's appointment as Foreign Secretary
stories.summary: Bond, the UK network for international development NGOs, welcomed Ed Miliband's appointment as Foreign Secretary, expressing hope for stronger UK leadership on global development and climate-linked aid.

stories.title: DWP benefits reform announcement expected this week from new PM
stories.summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham has confirmed a major DWP welfare reform announcement is coming shortly, with media reports suggesting changes to the benefits system are a priority for the new government. Details are expected to include cost-of-living support measures.

stories.title: DEFRA's 30×30 delivery plan backed by £37m for National Parks
stories.summary: DEFRA's plan to protect 30% of UK land and sea by 2030 has received £37 million in funding to support National Parks and landscapes in their nature recovery efforts. However, environmental campaigners argue the new strategy remains underpowered relative to the scale of the challenge.

stories.title: Environment Agency closes illegal waste site in Wolsingham
stories.summary: The Environment Agency has closed down a waste site in Wolsingham, County Durham, following reports of illegal waste dumping. The closure is part of ongoing enforcement action against unlicensed waste operations.

stories.title: UK Government launches Youth Climate and Nature Panel
stories.summary: The UK Government is inviting young people aged 16–18 to join its new Youth Climate and Nature Panel to help shape future policy. The initiative is linked to DEFRA's broader nature recovery agenda.

stories.title: Russian warship carries out live-fire exercise 40 miles off Plymouth
stories.summary: A Russian warship, shadowed by a Royal Navy vessel, conducted a live-fire artillery exercise just 40 nautical miles south of Plymouth. The incident has raised concerns about Russian military activity in UK waters.

stories.title: Hundreds of new military family homes completed at Gloucestershire barracks
stories.summary: The MoD has announced the completion of hundreds of new family homes at a Gloucestershire barracks, as part of its drive to transform Armed Forces housing. The project is a key element of the MoD's commitment to improving conditions for service personnel.

stories.title: Farnborough International Airshow 2026 invitees published
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has published the list of invitees for the Farnborough International Airshow (FIA) 2026, with the event serving as a key platform for UK defence industry engagement and export deals.

stories.title: Andy Burnham promises to be a pro-business prime minister
stories.summary: Incoming PM Andy Burnham has pledged a pro-business agenda, with Bloomberg reporting he is set to set out what this means for investment, trade policy, and the role of the Department for Business and Trade. A Bloomberg discussion on his economic approach is scheduled for 21 July.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: Tech leaders warn against dismantling DSIT as Burnham eyes Whitehall overhaul
stories.summary: Technology sector leaders have urged incoming PM Andy Burnham not to abolish or weaken the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) as part of any Whitehall restructuring. Concern is mounting in the tech industry that DSIT's mandate could be diminished.

stories.title: Project Gigabit contracts data published for July 2026
stories.summary: Building Digital UK (BDUK), operating under DSIT, has published its monthly contract-level data on the progress of Project Gigabit rollout across the UK, tracking premises contracted and built under the broadband infrastructure programme.

stories.title: UK pumps £1bn into AI supercomputing with Isambard-AI and Dawn
stories.summary: The UK has announced a £1 billion investment in AI supercomputing infrastructure, including the Isambard-AI and Dawn facilities, aiming to multiply the country's AI computing capacity by 20 times. The investment underlines DSIT's ambition to make the UK a global AI leader.

stories.title: Rising numbers of younger midwives quitting NHS in England, data shows
stories.summary: New data reported by The Guardian shows an increasing trend of younger midwives leaving the NHS in England, raising concerns about workforce sustainability and maternity service capacity. The trend is linked to workload pressures and pay disputes.

stories.title: Palantir isn't the only option for the NHS, say critics
stories.summary: A Guardian analysis argues that the NHS should not be locked into relying solely on Palantir for its data platform, urging NHS England to explore alternative digital infrastructure providers as a matter of strategic independence.

stories.title: NHS Wales faces critical shortage of radiologists and oncologists
stories.summary: A Royal College of Radiologists workforce census has found that NHS Wales is short of 88 clinical radiologists and 13 clinical oncologists, posing a serious threat to cancer diagnosis and treatment services in Wales.

stories.title: Fraudster ordered to repay £5m to Royal Mail or face further jail time
stories.summary: A fraudster who orchestrated a £70 million postal scam over nine years — acquiring properties and luxury cars — has been ordered to repay more than £5 million to Royal Mail or face additional imprisonment. The case is one of the largest fraud recovery actions against the postal service.
